{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_89ec6348b1bb4e088751907c","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Pluck Debug Flags and Logging Configuration\n2\t\n3\t## Overview\n4\t\n5\tPluck is the primary strand in NEEDLE (the bead processing system) that handles bead selection and filtering. It uses Rust's `tracing` framework for structured logging, controlled via the `RUST_LOG` environment variable.\n6\t\n7\t## Environment Variable: RUST_LOG\n8\t\n9\tThe `RUST_LOG` environment variable controls logging verbosity and scope. It uses the format:\n10\t\n11\t```bash\n12\tRUST_LOG=module_path=log_level,module_path2=log_level2\n13\t```\n14\t\n15\t### Log Levels (in order of verbosity)\n16\t\n17\t- `error` - Errors only\n18\t- `warn` - Warnings and errors  \n19\t- `info` - Informational messages (default)\n20\t- `debug` - Detailed debugging information\n21\t- `trace` - Most detailed level (includes function entry/exit)\n22\t\n23\t### Module Paths for Pluck\n24\t\n25\t| Module Path | Description |\n26\t|-------------|-------------|\n27\t| `needle::strand::pluck` | **Primary Pluck strand** - filtering decisions, candidate selection |\n28\t| `needle::strand` | General strand operations |\n29\t| `needle::bead_store` | Bead store queries and operations |\n30\t| `needle::worker` | Worker state machine and processing |\n31\t| `needle::dispatch` | Agent dispatch and execution |\n32\t\n33\t## Recommended Debug Configurations\n34\t\n35\t### For Pluck Filtering Decisions (Recommended)\n36\t\n37\t```bash\n38\texport RUST_LOG=\"needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug,needle::dispatch=debug\"\n39\t```\n40\t\n41\tThis provides:\n42\t- **Trace** level for Pluck strand (most detailed filtering decisions)\n43\t- **Debug** level for related components\n44\t- Full visibility into which beads are filtered and why\n45\t\n46\t### For Maximum Verbosity\n47\t\n48\t```bash\n49\texport RUST_LOG=\"trace\"\n50\t```\n51\t\n52\tEnables trace-level logging across all modules.\n53\t\n54\t### For Pluck-Specific Debugging\n55\t\n56\t```bash\n57\texport RUST_LOG=\"needle::strand::pluck=debug\"\n58\t```\n59\t\n60\tOnly enables debug logging for the Pluck strand itself.\n61\t\n62\t## Key Debug Events Logged\n63\t\n64\tThe Pluck strand logs the following filtering decisions at **debug** level:\n65\t\n66\t1. **Evaluation Start**\n67\t   - Configuration (exclude_labels, split_threshold)\n68\t   - Initial candidate query\n69\t\n70\t2. **Label Filtering**\n71\t   - Number of beads excluded by label\n72\t   - Individual excluded bead IDs and reasons\n73\t   - Remaining candidate count\n74\t\n75\t3. **Status/Assignee Filtering**\n76\t   - Removal of in-progress beads\n77\t   - Removal of stale assignee beads\n78\t\n79\t4. **Candidate Sorting**\n80\t   - Total count being sorted\n81\t   - First candidate details (priority, created_at)\n82\t\n83\t5. **Split Trigger Evaluation**\n84\t   - Failure count for first candidate\n85\t   - Split threshold comparison\n86\t   - Split trigger decision\n87\t\n88\t6. **Final Results**\n89\t   - Number of candidates returned\n90\t   - List of candidate IDs\n91\t   - NoWork reasons\n92\t\n93\t## Example Usage\n94\t\n95\t### Manual Debug Session\n96\t\n97\t```bash\n98\t# Set environment variable\n99\texport RUST_LOG=\"needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug,needle::dispatch=debug\"\n100\t\n101\t# Run NEEDLE\n102\tneedle run -w /path/to/workspace -c 1\n103\t```\n104\t\n105\t### Using the Capture Script\n106\t\n107\tARMOR includes a helper script for capturing debug output:\n108\t\n109\t```bash\n110\t./capture-pluck-debug.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log 1\n111\t```\n112\t\n113\tThis automatically sets `RUST_LOG` and captures output to a file.\n114\t\n115\t### Analyzing Debug Output\n116\t\n117\t```bash\n118\t# View Pluck filtering decisions\n119\tgrep -i 'pluck' output.log\n120\t\n121\t# View filtering exclusions\n122\tgrep -i 'exclude' output.log\n123\t\n124\t# View candidate selection\n125\tgrep -i 'candidate' output.log\n126\t\n127\t# View split trigger events\n128\tgrep -i 'split' output.log\n129\t```\n130\t\n131\t## Filtering Decision Details\n132\t\n133\tPluck performs the following filtering operations (each logged separately):\n134\t\n135\t1. **Label Exclusion** - Removes beads with excluded labels (default: `deferred`, `human`, `blocked`)\n136\t2. **Status Filter** - Removes beads in `InProgress` status\n137\t3. **Assignee Filter** - Removes `Open` beads with stale assignees\n138\t4. **Sorting** - Orders by `(priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)`\n139\t5. **Split Check** - Triggers split if failure-count exceeds threshold\n140\t\n141\t## Configuration Files\n142\t\n143\tThe `.needle.yaml` file in each workspace can configure Pluck behavior:\n144\t\n145\t```yaml\n146\texclude_labels:\n147\t  - It uses Rust's `tracing` framework for structured logging, controlled via the `RUST_LOG` environment variable.\n\n## Environment Variable: RUST_LOG\n\nThe `RUST_LOG` environment variable controls logging verbosity and scope. It uses the format:\n\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=module_path=log_level,module_path2=log_level2\n```\n\n### Log Levels (in order of verbosity)\n\n- `error` - Errors only\n- `warn` - Warnings and errors  \n- `info` - Informational messages (default)\n- `debug` - Detailed debugging information\n- `trace` - Most detailed level (includes function entry/exit)\n\n### Module Paths for Pluck\n\n| Module Path | Description |\n|-------------|-------------|\n| `needle::strand::pluck` | **Primary Pluck strand** - filtering decisions, candidate selection |\n| `needle::strand` | General strand operations |\n| `needle::bead_store` | Bead store queries and operations |\n| `needle::worker` | Worker state machine and processing |\n| `needle::dispatch` | Agent dispatch and execution |\n\n## Recommended Debug Configurations\n\n### For Pluck Filtering Decisions (Recommended)\n\n```bash\nexport RUST_LOG=\"needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug,needle::dispatch=debug\"\n```\n\nThis provides:\n- **Trace** level for Pluck strand (most detailed filtering decisions)\n- **Debug** level for related components\n- Full visibility into which beads are filtered and why\n\n### For Maximum Verbosity\n\n```bash\nexport RUST_LOG=\"trace\"\n```\n\nEnables trace-level logging across all modules.\n\n### For Pluck-Specific Debugging\n\n```bash\nexport RUST_LOG=\"needle::strand::pluck=debug\"\n```\n\nOnly enables debug logging for the Pluck strand itself.\n\n## Key Debug Events Logged\n\nThe Pluck strand logs the following filtering decisions at **debug** level:\n\n1. **Evaluation Start**\n   - Configuration (exclude_labels, split_threshold)\n   - Initial candidate query\n\n2. **Label Filtering**\n   - Number of beads excluded by label\n   - Individual excluded bead IDs and reasons\n   - Remaining candidate count\n\n3. **Status/Assignee Filtering**\n   - Removal of in-progress beads\n   - Removal of stale assignee beads\n\n4. **Candidate Sorting**\n   - Total count being sorted\n   - First candidate details (priority, created_at)\n\n5. **Split Trigger Evaluation**\n   - Failure count for first candidate\n   - Split threshold comparison\n   - Split trigger decision\n\n6. **Final Results**\n   - Number of candidates returned\n   - List of candidate IDs\n   - NoWork reasons\n\n## Example Usage\n\n### Manual Debug Session\n\n```bash\n# Set environment variable\nexport RUST_LOG=\"needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug,needle::dispatch=debug\"\n\n# Run NEEDLE\nneedle run -w /path/to/workspace -c 1\n```\n\n### Using the Capture Script\n\nARMOR includes a helper script for capturing debug output:\n\n```bash\n./capture-pluck-debug.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log 1\n```\n\nThis automatically sets `RUST_LOG` and captures output to a file.\n\n### Analyzing Debug Output\n\n```bash\n# View Pluck filtering decisions\ngrep -i 'pluck' output.log\n\n# View filtering exclusions\ngrep -i 'exclude' output.log\n\n# View candidate selection\ngrep -i 'candidate' output.log\n\n# View split trigger events\ngrep -i 'split' output.log\n```\n\n## Filtering Decision Details\n\nPluck performs the following filtering operations (each logged separately):\n\n1. **Label Exclusion** - Removes beads with excluded labels (default: `deferred`, `human`, `blocked`)\n2. **Status Filter** - Removes beads in `InProgress` status\n3. **Assignee Filter** - Removes `Open` beads with stale assignees\n4. **Sorting** - Orders by `(priority ASC, created_at ASC, id ASC)`\n5. **Split Check** - Triggers split if failure-count exceeds threshold\n\n## Configuration Files\n\nThe `.needle.yaml` file in each workspace can configure Pluck behavior:\n\n```yaml\nexclude_labels:\n  - deferred\n  - human  \n  - blocked\n```\n\nCustom exclude labels override the defaults and are logged at debug level.\n\n## Tracing Instrumentation\n\nPluck uses structured tracing with the following fields:\n\n- `strand` - Always \"pluck\"\n- `exclude_labels` - Configured exclusion labels\n- `split_threshold` - Failure count threshold for split trigger\n- `bead_id` - Specific bead being evaluated\n- `count` - Number of candidates\n- `candidates` - List of candidate IDs\n\n## Output Format\n\nDebug output uses structured logging (JSON when OTLP is enabled, text format otherwise):\n\n```\n2026-07-09T00:32:15.123Z DEBUG needle::strand::pluck:pluck_strand: Pluck strand evaluation starting\n  exclude_labels=[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"] split_threshold=3\n```\n\n## Related Documentation\n\n- **Pluck Source**: `/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/strand/pluck.rs`\n- **Strand Architecture**: NEEDLE strand system documentation\n- **Capture Script**: `capture-pluck-debug.sh` in ARMOR workspace\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### No debug output appearing\n\n1. Verify `RUST_LOG` is set: `echo $RUST_LOG`\n2. Check stderr is being captured: `needle run ... 2> output.log`\n3. Ensure tracing subscriber is initialized (automatic in needle CLI)\n\n### Too much output\n\nNarrow the scope to just the Pluck strand:\n```bash\nexport RUST_LOG=\"needle::strand::pluck=debug\"\n```\n\n### Missing filtering decisions\n\nEnsure trace level is enabled for detailed filtering logic:\n```bash\nexport RUST_LOG=\"needle::strand::pluck=trace\"\n```","numLines":200,"startLine":1,"totalLines":200}}}
